question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() reads filename only from the first signature (when filename == null) and never validates that all subsequent signatures share the same filename, allowing a fee-modified stream file's signatures to be mixed with a legitimate file's signatures to satisfy the consensus threshold?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where the first signature has status DOWNLOADED (not VERIFIED), causing filename and totalStake to be set from an unverified signature, while subsequent VERIFIED signatures use that potentially attacker-controlled totalStake to compute a manipulated consensus threshold for a fee-modified stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that stakeRequiredForConsensus is computed from the first signature regardless of its status (DOWNLOADED, NOT_FOUND, etc.) to inject a first signature with a manipulated node totalStake, lowering the consensus bar for a fee-modified stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where all nodes have stake=0 but totalStake > 0, causing the fallback to node-count-based consensus (as seen in tests with zero stakes), and then supply enough zero-stake node signatures on a fee-modified stream file to meet the 1/3 node count threshold?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the zero-stake fallback behavior (where totalStake=0 causes getStakeRequiredForConsensus to throw, but individual node stake=0 with totalStake>0 allows consensus by count) to craft a scenario where a fee-modified stream file is accepted by count-based consensus with fewer nodes than the stake-based threshold would require?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where the same node ID appears multiple times with different stake values, causing the stake accumulation loop to sum all instances and artificially inflate the stake for a fee-modified stream file's hash, bypassing the intended per-node stake limit?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum collection size check in validate() to supply an unbounded number of signatures, causing CPU exhaustion in the nested loops (outer loop over signatureHashMap keys, inner loop over validatedSignatures) and creating a denial-of-service that halts fee record processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where fileHash bytes are crafted to produce a hex string that collides with a legitimate node's fileHashAsHex, causing the fee-modified signature to be grouped with legitimate signatures in signatureHashMap and the combined stake to satisfy the consensus threshold?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not verify the cryptographic integrity of fileHash itself (only that enough nodes signed the same hash) to supply a pre-image attack on the SHA-384 hash of a fee-modified stream file, causing it to match the hash of a legitimate file and pass consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mutable setStatus() call on StreamFileSignature objects inside validate() to cause a race condition in a concurrent environment, where a fee-modified signature's status is set to CONSENSUS_REACHED by one thread while another thread is still evaluating it, leading to incorrect fee data being persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where the node's getStake() returns a negative value, causing the stake accumulation to decrease below zero, making canReachConsensus() return false even when legitimate nodes have sufficient stake, resulting in a denial-of-service on fee validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not check whether the ConsensusNode objects in the signatures are from the official address book, allowing injection of phantom nodes with arbitrary stake values that push a fee-modified stream file's hash over the consensus threshold?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where all signatures have the same node reference (same ConsensusNode object), causing the stake to be summed once per signature rather than once per node, and allowing a single node's stake to be multiplied by the number of duplicate signatures to meet the consensus threshold for a fee-modified stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a check that the signatures collection contains at least one signature per expected network node, allowing a fee-modified stream file to pass consensus with signatures from only a small subset of nodes if those nodes happen to hold >= 1/3 of the total stake?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not validate the stream file's content (only its hash and signature count) to supply a stream file where transaction fees are modified but the hash matches what enough nodes signed, if those nodes were compromised or the hash was pre-computed from the modified content?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where the first signature's node has totalStake=1 and stake=1, while all other signatures have status DOWNLOADED, causing the consensus threshold to be 1 and a single VERIFIED signature on a fee-modified stream file to satisfy consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the consensusRatio check uses BigDecimal.ZERO.equals() (scale-sensitive) rather than compareTo(BigDecimal.ZERO) == 0 to craft a scenario where a consensusRatio of 0.0 (scale 1) does not trigger the bypass, causing the validator to attempt consensus with a near-zero threshold that a single fee-modified signature can satisfy?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getStakeRequiredForConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where totalStake is 1 and consensusRatio is the default 1/3, causing getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to return ceiling(1 * 0.333) = 1, so that a single node with stake=1 can validate a fee-modified stream file as having reached consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of node deduplication in the stake accumulation loop to supply N copies of the same VERIFIED signature (same node, same fileHash) for a fee-modified stream file, causing stake to be summed N times and the consensus threshold to be met with a single real node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where the fileHash is an empty byte array, causing getFileHashAsHex() to return an empty string, and then exploit HashMultimap grouping under the empty-string key to aggregate multiple fee-modified signatures that collectively meet the consensus threshold?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not enforce a minimum number of distinct nodes in the consensus group to allow a single high-stake node (holding >= 1/3 of total stake) to unilaterally validate a fee-modified stream file, bypassing the multi-node consensus intent?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where the node's getTotalStake() returns a value much smaller than the actual network total stake, causing getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to compute an artificially low threshold that a small number of fee-modified signatures can satisfy?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not check the stream file's consensus timestamp against expected ranges to inject a fee-modified stream file with a future or past timestamp that bypasses temporal validation checks downstream, causing incorrect fee records to be persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where the first signature has a null status, causing a NullPointerException in the status comparison (signature.getStatus() == StreamFileSignature.SignatureStatus.VERIFIED) that crashes the validation pipeline and prevents fee records from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not validate the SignatureType of each StreamFileSignature to inject signatures with an unsupported or null SignatureType, bypassing the SHA_384_WITH_RSA verification requirement and allowing fee-modified stream files to be accepted with weaker or no cryptographic guarantees?
